w:r.w:t: This draft includes only the features related to Operator Staff, Operator Admin, System Admin, shared authentication/access, notifications, RAG administration, and relevant non-screen automation. Passenger mobile, driver mobile, and assistant operation-only features are excluded from this section because they are outside the implemented/reporting scope provided by the user.
Public operator representative submits registration request; System Admin reviews the request.
Allow a bus operator to enter the platform after email verification and admin approval.
Operator registration page and System Admin operator approval screen.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Operator Registration & Approval.
Company name, representative information, contact email, phone number, business registration number, tax code, address.
Required fields must not be empty; email and phone must be valid; business registration number and tax code must not duplicate existing operators.
New operator starts in pending approval; approved operator can access dashboard; rejected operator receives rejection reason.
Normal: registration is submitted, email is verified, admin approves, operator dashboard becomes accessible. Abnormal: duplicated tax code, invalid OTP, expired OTP, or admin rejection.
User clicks Login button from Operator Web or System Admin Web.
Operator Staff, Operator Admin, System Admin
Authenticate user and route them to the correct dashboard according to role.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Login.
Email must be valid; password must not be blank; account must be active and role must be allowed to access selected portal.
Inactive, locked, or unauthorized accounts cannot sign in.
Normal: valid credentials redirect user to dashboard. Abnormal: wrong password, locked account, inactive operator, or insufficient permission.
Staff user opens invitation link sent after account creation.
Driver/Assistant accounts may exist in system, but this section covers accounts created by Operator/Admin management screens.
Let newly created personnel set their first password securely.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Set Initial Password.
Invitation token, new password, confirm password.
Token must be valid and unexpired; password must follow security policy; confirm password must match.
Initial password token can be used once only.
Normal: password is set and account becomes active. Abnormal: expired token, used token, weak password, or mismatch confirmation.
User clicks Forgot Password on login screen.
Operator Staff, Operator Admin, System Admin
Allow user to reset password through email-based verification.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Forgot Password.
Email, verification code/token, new password, confirm password.
Email must exist; token must be valid; new password must satisfy password policy.
Reset token expires after configured duration.
Normal: user receives token and resets password. Abnormal: invalid email, expired token, invalid token, or weak password.
User clicks Logout from dashboard/profile menu.
Operator Staff, Operator Admin, System Admin

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Logout.
Access token/session should no longer be usable after logout.
Normal: user is redirected to login page. Abnormal: expired session still clears local session and returns to login.
Operator Staff opens Station/Route/Stop management menu.
Create and maintain stations, routes, stops, stop order, return routes, and alternative routes under operator scope.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Stations, Routes, and Stops.
Station name, city, address, coordinates, route name, origin, destination, stop list, stop order.
Required fields must be provided; stop order must be unique; origin and destination should be different; route must belong to current operator.
Operator can only manage data within its own scope.
Normal: create/update route and stops successfully. Abnormal: duplicate stop order, missing coordinates, invalid station, or unauthorized operator access.
Operator Staff opens Trip Schedule management menu.
Create and update trip schedules and driver schedule assignments.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Trip Schedules.
Route, vehicle, driver, assistant, departure time, arrival estimate, fare, recurrence setting.
Departure time must be in the future; vehicle and personnel must be available; route must be active.
Subscription limits and resource availability may block creation.
Normal: trip schedule is saved and available for operation. Abnormal: vehicle conflict, driver conflict, route inactive, or subscription limit reached.
Operator Staff opens Booking monitoring screen.
View passenger bookings, booking status, and booking details under operator scope.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Monitor Bookings.
Booking code, trip, passenger contact, seats, pickup/dropoff, payment and booking status.
Search/filter inputs must be valid; booking data is read-only for operator staff.
Operator staff can monitor but should not create passenger bookings manually.
Normal: booking list and details are displayed. Abnormal: no records found or user attempts to access another operator’s booking.
Operator Staff opens Parcel Operations screen.
Track parcel requests, parcel status, and parcel-related operational cases.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Monitor Parcel Operations.
Parcel code, trip, sender/recipient contact, route stops, size, weight, fee, current status.
Parcel must belong to operator’s trip; filters should use valid dates/status.
Operator monitors parcel operations but sender creates parcel request from passenger side.
Normal: parcel list and status history are displayed. Abnormal: parcel not found, unauthorized access, or invalid filter.
Operator Staff/Admin opens extra-large parcel review queue.
Approve or reject parcel requests that require manual review and pricing.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Review Extra-large Parcels.
Parcel detail, images if available, declared weight/size, requested trip, manual fee, decision reason.
Manual fee is required when approving; rejection reason is recommended/required depending on UI rule.
Extra-large parcels cannot proceed to payment until approved.
Normal: operator approves with fee or rejects with reason. Abnormal: invalid fee, already processed request, insufficient capacity.
Operator Staff opens Fleet Monitoring dashboard.
Monitor active trips, vehicle locations, delays, incidents, and operation alerts.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Monitor Fleet Operations.
Active trip list, vehicle location, trip status, ETA, delay alerts, incident alerts.
User can only see trips under the current operator.
Real-time data depends on driver GPS broadcasting and tracking service availability.
Normal: active trips and alerts display on dashboard. Abnormal: GPS unavailable, trip not active, or tracking service unavailable.
Operator Staff opens disruption handling from trip/alert detail.
Handle delays, incidents, route changes, vehicle substitution, cancellation, and passenger impact actions.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Trip Disruptions.
Trip, disruption type, reason, affected passengers, new schedule/route/vehicle, notification content.
Reason is required; selected action must be allowed for current trip status.
Passenger impact should trigger notification and confirmation flow when required.
Normal: disruption action is saved and affected users are notified. Abnormal: invalid trip status, unavailable replacement vehicle, or failed notification.
Operator Staff opens shuttle support screen for eligible pickup scenarios.
Dispatch or monitor shuttle support for pickup at stations where shuttle is enabled.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Dispatch Shuttle Support.
Trip, passenger pickup information, station, shuttle status, assigned support staff/vehicle if applicable.
Shuttle must be enabled for station/operator plan; passenger pickup must be eligible.
Shuttle support is only available for configured stations and enabled subscription/module.
Normal: shuttle support is assigned/monitored. Abnormal: unsupported station, unavailable shuttle, or disabled module.
Operator Admin opens Company Profile / Policies menu.
Update company information, contact details, operated stations, and operating policies.
Company profile and policy configuration screen.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Company Profile and Policies.
Company name, logo, address, contact phone/email, cancellation policy, operating notes.
Required company fields must not be empty; policy tiers must be valid and non-overlapping.
Changes apply only to current operator.
Normal: profile and policy are updated. Abnormal: invalid policy tier, missing contact data, or unauthorized role.
Operator Admin opens Personnel Management menu.
Create and manage driver, assistant, operator staff, and operator admin accounts.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Personnel Accounts.
Full name, email, phone, role, status, assigned operator.
Email/phone must be valid; role must be allowed; duplicate email is not allowed.
New staff accounts use invitation/set-initial-password flow.
Normal: account is created and invitation is sent. Abnormal: duplicate email, invalid role, subscription staff limit reached.
Operator Admin opens Vehicle Management menu.
Manage vehicles, vehicle types, vehicle status, and seat layout information.
Vehicle and seat layout management screen.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Vehicles and Seat Layouts.
License plate/display name, vehicle type, seat layout, total seats, vehicle status.
License plate/display name should be unique under operator; seat layout must match total seats.
Retired or maintenance vehicles cannot be assigned to new trips.
Normal: vehicle and layout are saved. Abnormal: duplicate vehicle, invalid layout, or vehicle already assigned.
3.4.4 Manage Cargo Capacity and Seat Availability
Operator Admin opens Vehicle/Trip capacity and seat availability management.
Configure cargo capacity and manually disable or enable seats when required.
Cargo capacity and seat availability screen.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Cargo Capacity and Seat Availability.
Maximum cargo weight/volume, trip seat availability, disable/enable reason.
Capacity must be positive; seat must exist; reason is required for manual disable.
Manual disable only removes seat from available pool; it is not a manual booking sale.
Normal: capacity or seat availability is updated. Abnormal: invalid capacity, unavailable seat, already booked seat.
View or manage SaaS subscription plan, usage limits, and related invoices.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Operator Subscription.
Current plan, plan limits, usage counters, invoices, module availability.
Upgrade/downgrade requests must use active plan options; payment may be required.
Exceeded plan limits block related resource creation.
Normal: subscription information is displayed or plan action is initiated. Abnormal: payment failure, inactive plan, or limit exceeded.
View wallet balance, pending revenue, settlement history, and wallet transactions.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Wallet and Settlement.
Wallet balance, pending hold amount, settlement status, transaction history.
Date/status filters must be valid; data is read-only except allowed payout actions if any.
Funds are held until settlement eligibility conditions are met.
Normal: wallet and settlement records are displayed. Abnormal: no records found or settlement still pending hold.
Operator Admin opens Voucher Consent module.
Accept or reject operator-funded voucher campaigns proposed by platform.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Voucher Consent.
Voucher code, discount value, validity period, applicable routes/operators, consent status, rejection reason.
Only Operator Admin can accept/reject; rejection reason may be required.
Accepted voucher applies to future eligible bookings only.
Normal: voucher consent is accepted or rejected. Abnormal: expired voucher, already processed consent, unauthorized staff role.
3.4.8 View Operator Dashboard and Export Reports
Operator Admin opens dashboard or report export menu.
View operational statistics, revenue reports, and export supported reports.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for View Operator Dashboard and Export Reports.
Date range, report type, filters, export format.
Date range must be valid; report type must be supported.
Operator only sees its own operational data.
Normal: dashboard displays statistics or exports report. Abnormal: no data, invalid date range, or export generation failure.
System Admin opens Operator Management menu.
Create, approve, reject, suspend, update, or delete bus operators.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Operators.
Operator profile, business registration number, tax code, contact information, status, approval/rejection reason.
Business registration and tax code must be unique; approval/rejection action requires a valid target operator.
Only System Admin can approve or suspend operators.
Normal: operator is approved/rejected/suspended/updated. Abnormal: duplicate operator, missing reason, or invalid status transition.
System Admin opens User Management / Activity Logs.
Manage user accounts, lock or unlock users, and review activity logs.
User management and activity log screen.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Users and Activity Logs.
User profile, role, status, lock reason, activity action, timestamp, IP/user agent if available.
Role and status must be valid; lock/unlock reason may be required.
System Admin can manage accounts across platform scope.
Normal: user list/logs display and account status is updated. Abnormal: user not found, invalid role, or protected admin account action.
System Admin opens Platform Station Management.
Normalize station data, merge duplicate stations, update station information, or deactivate invalid records.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Platform Stations.
Station name, address, city/province, coordinates, duplicate station, merge target.
Coordinates and merge target must be valid; cannot merge station into itself.
Merging should preserve operator station links.
Normal: station is normalized, merged, or deactivated. Abnormal: invalid coordinate, duplicate merge conflict, station in active use without handling.
Create and manage platform-level voucher campaigns and monitor operator voucher consent status.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Platform Vouchers.
Voucher code, type, value, min order, max discount, validity, usage limits, funding type, target operators/routes.
Voucher code must be unique; date range must be valid; discount value must be positive and within allowed bounds.
VietRide-funded vouchers apply directly; operator-funded vouchers require operator consent.
Normal: voucher is created/updated and consent tracking is available. Abnormal: duplicate code, invalid date, invalid funding configuration.
System Admin opens Subscription Plan Management.
Create and manage subscription plans and default plan settings.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Subscription Plans.
Plan name, price, limits, module flags, active status, default flag.
Plan name must be unique; limits/prices must be non-negative; only one default active plan should be configured.
Inactive plans cannot be assigned to new operators.
Normal: plan is created/updated/deactivated. Abnormal: duplicate plan, invalid limit, or default plan missing.
System Admin opens Platform Wallet/Settlement menu.
Review platform wallet transactions and settlement information across operators.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Platform Wallet and Settlement.
Operator, trip, settlement status, platform wallet transactions, settlement amount, date range.
Filters must be valid; manual settlement requires eligible settlement record.
System Admin can trigger manual settlement when allowed.
Normal: settlement records display or manual settlement is triggered. Abnormal: settlement not eligible, already settled, or transaction failure.
3.5.7 Manage RAG Knowledge Base and Conversation Audit
System Admin opens RAG Knowledge Base / Audit module.
Manage RAG documents and review AI conversation logs for monitoring and quality control.
RAG document management and conversation audit screen.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage RAG Knowledge Base and Conversation Audit.
Document file, title, role permission, approval status, conversation log metadata.
Only supported file types are accepted; role permission must be valid; approval action requires pending document.
Approved documents may trigger ingestion automation.
Normal: document is approved/rejected/archived and audit logs are reviewed. Abnormal: unsupported file, ingestion failure, unauthorized access.
System Admin opens System Settings menu.
Configure platform-level settings and operation parameters.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Configure System Settings.
Timeout values, notification parameters, platform configuration flags, operational thresholds.
Settings must be within allowed ranges; risky changes may require confirmation.
Normal: setting is saved and applied. Abnormal: invalid value, missing required setting, or conflicting configuration.
System Admin opens Platform Reports dashboard.
View aggregate platform reports, revenue statistics, operator performance, and system activity.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for View Platform Reports.
Date range, operator filter, metric type, export format if supported.
Date range must be valid; selected operator/metric must exist.
Reports aggregate data across operators and should respect admin access only.
Normal: dashboard displays aggregate metrics. Abnormal: no data, invalid filter, or export failure.
User opens notification center from dashboard.
Operator Staff, Operator Admin, System Admin
View notifications, alerts, and operational updates relevant to current role.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for View Notifications and Alerts.
Notification type, title, message, related entity, read/unread status, created time.
User can only view notifications permitted for their role and operator/platform scope.
Important operational events should create persistent notification records.
Normal: user reads notification list and opens related item. Abnormal: related item not found or notification outside permission scope.
Operator Staff, Operator Admin, System Admin
Navigate from notification to related trip, booking, parcel, incident, operator, settlement, or voucher detail.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Open Related Notification Detail.
Notification id and related entity reference.
Related entity must exist and be accessible by user role.
If target is unavailable, system should show a clear error message.
Normal: user is redirected to related detail screen. Abnormal: deleted target, unauthorized target, or stale notification.
System consumes domain events and dispatches push or in-app notifications asynchronously.
Deliver operational updates without blocking core business flow.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Notification Dispatch.
Event payload, recipient role/user/operator, notification type, delivery channel.
Event must be valid; recipient resolution must succeed; retry should be available for failed dispatch.
Notification dispatch is asynchronous and should not roll back main transaction.
Normal: notification is created/sent. Abnormal: push provider failure, invalid recipient, or retry to dead-letter queue.
Scheduled job runs according to configured timing.
Automatically generate trips from driver schedule assignments.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Trip Generation from Driver Schedule.
Driver schedule, route, vehicle, driver/assistant assignment, generated date range.
Schedule must be active; vehicle/personnel conflicts must be checked; subscription limit must be respected.
Generated trips should not duplicate existing trips for same schedule/date.
Normal: trips are generated. Abnormal: conflict, subscription limit exceeded, or inactive schedule.
Scheduled job evaluates eligible settlement records and processes weekly settlement.
Move eligible operator revenue from platform holding to operator wallet after hold period.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Trip Settlement Automation.
Settlement records, ledger entries, operator wallet, platform wallet, settlement status.
Settlement must be eligible; final net amount must be positive; wallet update must be transactional.
Late refunds should be reflected before final payout.
Normal: settlement is paid out and marked settled. Abnormal: zero/negative settlement, wallet transaction failure, or already settled record.
Scheduled job checks subscription status periodically.
Send expiry warnings and mark expired subscriptions when needed.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Subscription Expiry and Warning.
Operator subscription, expiry date, warning thresholds, operator admin recipients.
Subscription dates must be valid; warning should not spam repeatedly.
Expired subscription may restrict creation of new resources.
Normal: warning is sent or subscription is expired. Abnormal: missing subscription, invalid dates, or notification failure.
Monitoring system calls health check endpoints.
Verify service availability for monitoring and deployment readiness.

The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Service Health Check.
Service status, dependencies status if provided, timestamp.
Endpoint should respond quickly and not expose sensitive data.
Health check supports uptime monitoring and deployment verification.
Normal: service returns healthy. Abnormal: dependency unavailable or service returns unhealthy.
Function trigger: User opens Profile from the Manager or System Admin route.
Purpose: Let an authenticated user view and update personal profile and address information shown in the web profile screen.

Data processing: The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for View and Update User Profile.
Data requirement: First name, last name, email, phone, bio, country, city/state, postal code, and tax ID.
Validation: Editable fields must use valid contact/address formats; email should remain unique when backend persistence is enabled.
Business rules: User can edit own profile information only; save/cancel actions must not alter role or permission scope.
Normal and abnormal cases: Normal: user edits profile or address and saves changes. Abnormal: invalid contact data, failed save, or unauthorized profile access.
Function trigger: User lands on Manager Dashboard or System Admin Dashboard after successful login.
Purpose: Provide KPI cards, charts, operational summaries, and quick access to pending actions for the current role.
Interface: Manager dashboard and System Admin dashboard.

Data processing: The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for View Role Dashboard.
Data requirement: Revenue, bookings, fleet status, parcel status, user distribution, operator status, pending approvals, and export report options.
Validation: Dashboard data must respect role scope and date/filter selections.
Business rules: Manager dashboard shows operator-scoped operation metrics; System Admin dashboard shows platform-wide metrics and pending operator approvals.
Normal and abnormal cases: Normal: dashboard cards and charts load for the signed-in role. Abnormal: no data, chart data unavailable, or role mismatch.
Function trigger: Operator Manager opens the Route ETA screen and reviews pending ETA adjustment requests.
Purpose: Review delay impact, adjusted ETA, affected passenger count, and approve or reject the ETA notification flow.
Interface: Route ETA request list and detail modal.

Data processing: The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Review Route ETA Adjustment Requests.
Data requirement: Request code, route, reason, adjusted ETA delay, affected passengers, extra request note, and request status.
Validation: Only pending requests can be approved or rejected; selected request must belong to the current operator scope.
Business rules: Approval marks the ETA request as approved and should send passenger notification; rejection marks it as rejected and stops notification dispatch.
Normal and abnormal cases: Normal: manager approves and sends notification or rejects the request. Abnormal: request already processed, invalid route scope, or notification failure.
Function trigger: Operator Manager opens Vehicle Layout Builder from the manager route.
Purpose: Create a vehicle seat layout visually and export/import layout data for vehicle configuration.
Interface: Vehicle Layout Builder with toolbox, canvas, and properties panel.

Data processing: The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Vehicle Layout Builder.
Data requirement: Vehicle name, plate number, vehicle type/model, seat list, seat type, position X/Y, rotation, color, enabled flag, rows, columns, and total capacity.
Validation: A vehicle must be selected before editing; seat labels should be unique within layout; imported JSON must match expected layout shape.
Business rules: Supported seat types include normal, VIP, bed, and driver; layout can be generated by rows/columns, edited by drag/drop, cleared, exported, and imported.
Normal and abnormal cases: Normal: manager creates vehicle, adds or generates seats, edits properties, and saves layout JSON. Abnormal: malformed import file, no selected vehicle, or empty layout.
Function trigger: Operator Manager opens Packages menu to view current package or purchase an available plan.
Purpose: Show active subscription usage, available packages, voucher discounts, payment method, and purchase history.
Interface: Operator package marketplace and purchase modal.

Data processing: The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Purchase and Monitor Operator Packages.
Data requirement: Current package, expiry date, vehicle/route usage, package price/duration/limits, voucher code, payment method, and purchase status.
Validation: Selected package and duration must be valid; voucher code must be recognized before discount is applied; payment method must be selected.
Business rules: Package purchase may use wallet or QR payment; usage limits from current package constrain operator resources.
Normal and abnormal cases: Normal: manager reviews usage and completes package purchase. Abnormal: invalid voucher, payment failure, inactive package, or usage limit exceeded.
3.7.6 Configure Operator Pricing, Booking, and Parcel Settings
Function trigger: Operator Manager opens Settings and edits pricing, booking, or parcel configuration tabs.
Purpose: Maintain operator-level fare modifiers, booking rules, parcel settings, and holiday pricing periods.
Interface: Manager settings tabs and holiday pricing modal.

Data processing: The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Configure Operator Pricing, Booking, and Parcel Settings.
Data requirement: Pricing configuration, booking limits, parcel rules, holiday pricing period name, date range, and adjustment value.
Validation: Numeric settings must be within allowed ranges; holiday period date range must be valid; required period fields must be present.
Business rules: Save applies current operator configuration; reset restores the last saved snapshot; holiday periods can be added or edited.
Normal and abnormal cases: Normal: manager edits settings and saves successfully. Abnormal: invalid numeric value, missing period data, overlapping period, or failed save.
Function trigger: System Admin opens Policies menu and manages policy records.
Purpose: Create, edit, activate/deactivate, and delete policies for operators and users.
Interface: System Admin policies list and policy create/edit modal.

Data processing: The system validates the request, checks user permission and operator/platform scope, performs the related create/update/read action, records audit or activity information where applicable, and returns success or error messages to the user.
Screen layout / mock-up placeholder: Insert screenshot or prototype for Manage Platform Policies.
Data requirement: Policy title, description, content, category, policy type, version, active status, and updated date.
Validation: Required policy fields must be provided; policy type must be for operator or for user; content must not be empty.
Business rules: Active policies are shown as effective; inactive policies remain stored but should not apply to new user/operator flows.
Normal and abnormal cases: Normal: admin creates or updates a policy and toggles active status. Abnormal: missing required fields, deleting wrong policy, or unauthorized admin action.
The following feature groups are intentionally excluded from this draft because the requested scope focuses on Operator/Manager Web and System Admin Web only:
